name: Godot Game Engine
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); completely free, no royalties

auth.notes: Local engine; no auth; GDNative/GDExtension for plugins

performance.notes: Vulkan/Metal rendering in Godot 4; excellent for 2D, good for 3D; not AAA-level

agent_readiness.notes: Open-source game engine with excellent 2D capabilities and good 3D (Godot 4). GDScript (Python-like, fast iteration). C# support. GDExtension for C++/Rust. Visual scripting. Node-based architecture (not ECS). Cross-platform: Windows, macOS, Linux, Android, iOS, WASM. Built-in editor, animation, physics, tilemaps, UI. Excellent documentation. Major Unity alternative since Unity's runtime fee controversy (2023). Growing rapidly.

gotchas: 3D less mature than Unity/Unreal for complex AAA content
GDScript is Godot-specific (not Python — similar but different)
Smaller asset store vs Unity Asset Store
Godot 4 broke many Godot 3 projects (major API changes)
best_when: 2D games (one of the best 2D engines available)
Indie games prioritizing free, open-source toolchain
avoid_when: AAA 3D games requiring maximum visual fidelity (use Unreal)
Teams with large existing Unity codebases
Console publishing requiring dedicated console support
summary: Godot is a free, open-source (MIT) game engine excellent for 2D and capable for 3D. GDScript (Python-like) enables fast iteration. Godot 4 added Vulkan rendering. Cross-platform including WASM. Built-in editor with animation, physics, tilemaps, and UI. The leading Unity alternative since 2023. Best for indie 2D games and developers wanting completely free tools without royalties.
